preamble: The shift from the voiced velar stop [ɡ] to the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] (gamma) is a classic form of lenition known as spirantization. It most frequently occurs in intervocalic or post-vocalic positions where the closure required for a stop is incomplete, allowing continuous voiced airflow.

examples.note: Historical spirantization of initial voiced stops in most Dutch dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
